rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) to anchor the hedonic-versus-utilitarian choice domain as an established decision problem, then incorporates that domain into its own unifying self- versus other-expression framework. Hedonic/utilitarian choices are not a throwaway mention: they are one of the core problem types repeatedly analyzed in the conceptual setup and empirical studies. Even though the citation appears with other literature, it meaningfully informs how the paper frames and positions its contribution across choice dilemmas.
relation_description: The 2011 paper treats Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as foundational prior work on hedonic versus utilitarian choice, and then reinterprets that established dilemma as one instance of a broader self- versus other-expression pattern tested across multiple contexts.
